inline_comments.message:
Please do not put issue IDs in the subject line, but as part of the footer in the form:

Issue: <ISSUE-KEY>

Additionally, please explain why this is being done.

See https://chris.beams.io/posts/git-commit/ on writing good commit messages.

Note: we have new checks that will be rolling in eventually that require following these guidelines.

This should have been done with the previous template as well but it wasn't caught previously. This shell hardcodes expectation of a given project with how it's configured. Instead of this it should be moved to a macro variable that is passed in as part of the job definition parameters.

So, something like:

shell: '{shell-step}'
This should have a trailing ':' and then an additional passed variable called shell-step which is the shell script to be executed.
